approved_decisions.risk_acceptance: business_values_identical_zero_mismatch; lineage ownership selected as aux

approved_decisions.execution_authorization_status: not_yet_authorized; future authorization must mention exact manifest delete guard

approved_decisions.authorized_scope: authorize Gate B Batch 2 remaining datasets consolidated read-only split planning only for adj_factor, prices, prices_limit, events, trade_status; no lake write

global_preconditions_before_any_write.requirement: Establish a fenced/quiescent window and re-run Gate A inventory on /home/hyde/data/quant-lab/data-lake immediately before the write batch, or record explicit drift risk acceptance.
For every dataset to be written, complete a per-dataset full-row exact duplicate profile or record explicit N/A with reason.
Write mode must be additive-only to new CR139 run-id candidate paths; in-place overwrite, delete, or append into legacy paths is forbidden.
Generate object manifest, pre-write snapshot, post-write snapshot, row/hash evidence, and rollback manifest for each approved write batch.
Gate C catalog/checksum metadata, Gate D pointer advance, and Gate E physical migration remain separate gates and are not part of Gate B write execution.

dedup_sort_policy.dedup_sort_rationale: This ordering is deterministic and prefers the newest source run, then newest availability timestamp, then stable lineage checksum as a tie-breaker. It is not sufficient for metadata-conflicting groups; those remain quarantine by default under D-GATEB-001. If any required sort field is absent, deterministic dedup is not allowed without a new reviewed sort policy.

batching.description: Small/static datasets with no full-row duplicates. bse_code_mapping and safe-5 have candidate writes verified; trade_calendar and stock_basic moved to Batch 1A-exact-dedup after full-row duplicate discovery.
Small/static datasets with exact full-row duplicates but no duplicate-key metadata conflict. Requires deterministic exact-dedup candidate planning before any write.
batching.requires: read-only exact-dedup candidate planning gate before write authorization

batching.description: Index membership/weight datasets with special handling: index_members requires exact full-row dedup; index_weights preserves mixed row-level PIT status. Both require explicit write authorization.
batching.gate_d_precondition: Before any future pointer advance, index_weights pit_incomplete rows must have publish/isolate/repair/waiver decision.

batching.description: Duplicate-key or events datasets; one dataset per authorization after full-row profile and dedup/quarantine review.

datasets.dedup_retain_rule: sort by _cr139_source_file ASC, _cr139_source_row_number ASC, then drop exact full-row duplicates keeping first
sort by _cr139_source_file ASC, _cr139_source_row_number ASC, then drop exact full-row duplicates keeping first
sort by _cr139_source_file ASC, _cr139_source_row_number ASC, then drop exact full-row duplicates keeping first

datasets.semantics_decision: preserve mixed row-level pit_status; do not collapse dataset-level truth to fully pit_available
datasets.reader_implication: Downstream readers must treat index_weights as weight observations with row-level PIT metadata. It must not substitute for index_members constituency membership and must not be used to claim complete PIT universe coverage where pit_incomplete rows are present.
datasets.gate_d_precondition: Before published pointer advance, decide whether the 300 pit_incomplete rows are publishable, isolated, repaired, or waived.

datasets.dedup_sort_rationale: Apply only to duplicate-key groups proven metadata-compatible by per-dataset full-row profile and owner approval; metadata-conflicting groups remain quarantine. The ordering prefers newest source run, newest availability timestamp, then stable lineage checksum as a deterministic tie-breaker.

duplicate_risk_stratification.routing_rationale: Duplicate-key rows are all non-identical by full-row fingerprint; no exact-copy dedup bucket is available.
duplicate_risk_stratification.counting_note: Derived as duplicate-key over-unique minus full-row fingerprint duplicate over-unique. This is a routing estimate for planning; future per-dataset write gates must do exact bucket materialization and pre/post-write content validation.
